commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=eddf84558f13f04c093b3c2f5ac73738511f72ba)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps plugin tool factory results at materialization so factories, `prepareArguments`, and `execute` run in the owning plugin runtime scope, with regression tests in `src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +153. Total +232 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: return a plugin tool object whose `name` or `parameters` are inherited or non-enumerable, then the PR wrapper's object spread drops them before validation. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can preserve the original plugin tool surface while keeping the intended callback scoping behavior.

target.title: fix(plugins): scope tool callbacks during materialization
target.body:
## Summary
- Scope plugin tool factory resolution plus `prepareArguments` and `execute` callbacks using the owning `PluginToolRegistration` metadata.
- Apply the wrapper at the shared materialization boundary, including cached descriptor execution, so direct registry producers use the same owner scope.
- Replace the deep extension-import regression shape from #87829 with fixture-based core tests.

Alternative implementation for #87829. Credit to @vincentkoc for the original report and patch direction.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` (65 tests passed)
- `pnpm lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`
- `pnpm format:check src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Plugin tool factories, `prepareArguments`, and `execute` callbacks now run under the owning plugin scope instead of inheriting the caller's current scope.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node/pnpm repo tooling, branch `codex/plugin-tool-callback-scope-materialization`, commit `eddf84558f`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`; `pnpm format:check src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: The focused Vitest file passed 65/65 tests and the two new regression tests observed factory, prepare, and execute phases under the plugin owner scope while restoring the outer scope after success and thrown prepare errors.
Observed result after fix: Both single-tool and array-tool plugin factories preserve the owner plugin scope through materialization and runtime callbacks.
What was not tested: A full changed gate on Blacksmith Testbox was attempted twice, but both runs failed during dependency install before project checks; PR CI is the remaining broad proof source.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
